title: Rich French Milk Chocolate Ratatouille
description: A paleo-friendly twist on the classic ratatouille, featuring bitter milk chocolate and a silky texture, served in a traditional tajine-clay vessel.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the eggplant, zucchini, bell peppers, and onion with the olive oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the vegetables in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast for 30 minutes.
While the vegetables are roasting, melt the milk chocolate in a double boiler over low heat.
Once the chocolate is melted, stir in the sodium alginate and let it cool to room temperature.
Using a syringe or spoon, drop small amounts of the chocolate mixture into a bath of cold water to create chocolate spheres.
After the vegetables have finished roasting, let them cool to room temperature.
To serve, place a portion of the roasted vegetables in the bottom of a tajine-clay vessel and top with a few chocolate spheres.
Garnish with fresh herbs, if desired.
